page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical Intel Xeon 8581C cores running at 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The instance is configured with 372 GB of system memory, providing 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core execution, image processing, and text processing. Multi-threaded operations, including Bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang, fall into the strong performance tier. Cryptographic and database operations also perform strongly, with Redis SET operations reaching 58.32 million operations per second. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks remains in the average tier, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is weak, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-48 offers a high-density memory configuration paired with dedicated compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the premium of ultra-high-end memory instances. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, software build pipelines, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture. Running at 2.3 GHz with a 260 MB L3 cache, the CPU supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs. In benchmark testing, the c4-highmem-48 demonstrates top-tier single-core, image, and text processing performance, alongside strong multi-core capabilities in software compilation and database operations. It delivers high throughput for Redis operations and static web serving. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated compute resources, this server is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, data processing pipelines, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server with 48 dedicated vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering strong performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 server combining high memory density with advanced Intel Xeon processing for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM (7.75 GB per vCPU)
Intel Xeon 8581C processor with AMX and AVX-512 support
Top-tier single-core, image, and text processing benchmarks
Strong performance in database operations and Redis throughput
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for in-memory databases and memory-intensive pipelines
